name: Antigone
description: Antigone, daughter of Oedipus and Jocasta, is a central figure in Greek tragedy, renowned for her unwavering moral conviction and defiance of unjust authority. Born into a family cursed by fate, she faced immense hardship, including her father's tragic downfall and her brothers' fratricidal conflict. When Creon, the king of Thebes, forbade the burial of her brother Polynices, branding him a traitor, Antigone chose to uphold divine law and familial duty over human decree. She bravely defied Creon's edict, performing the burial rites for her brother, knowing full well the consequences. Her act of civil disobedience, driven by a deep sense of justice and respect for the dead, led to her imprisonment and eventual death. Antigone's story is a powerful exploration of the conflict between individual conscience and state power, the importance of moral courage, and the enduring strength of familial bonds. She stands as a symbol of resistance against tyranny and a testament to the power of individual action in the face of injustice. Her tragic fate serves as a cautionary tale about the dangers of hubris and the importance of upholding moral principles, even in the face of death.
subtitle: Defiance, Duty, and Tragic Fate.
achievements: Upheld divine law and familial duty

keyMyths.title: The Burial of Polynices
keyMyths.description: Creon forbids the burial of Polynices, declaring him a traitor. Antigone, defying the king's edict, secretly buries her brother, prioritizing divine law and familial duty.

keyMyths.title: Antigone's Imprisonment
keyMyths.description: After being caught burying Polynices, Antigone is condemned to death by Creon. Despite pleas from Haemon, Creon remains resolute in his judgment, leading to devastating consequences.

keyMyths.title: Antigone's Death
keyMyths.description: Antigone takes her own life in her tomb, choosing death over submission to Creon's unjust rule. Her death triggers a series of tragic events, including the suicides of Haemon and Eurydice.

sources.author: Sophocles
sources.description: A classic Greek tragedy detailing Antigone's defiance of Creon.
sources.text: A classic Greek tragedy detailing Antigone's defiance of Creon.

sources.author: Euripides
sources.description: References to the Oedipus cycle and the conflict between Polynices and Eteocles.
sources.text: References to the Oedipus cycle and the conflict between Polynices and Eteocles.
